title: Monsoon Peruvian Pork Tenderloin Tacu-Tacu
description: A Peruvian-inspired twist on traditional tacu-tacu, featuring pork tenderloin, peanuts, and a creamy aji amarillo sauce. This dish combines the flavors of monsoon season with the comfort of a hearty meal.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes. Add the chopped bell pepper and cook until tender, about 4 minutes. Add the chopped aji amarillo peppers and cook for an additional 2 minutes.

Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute. Add the toasted peanuts and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Add the cumin, paprika, black pepper, and salt to the skillet and stir to combine.

Add the pork tenderloin to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Add the heavy cream and chicken broth to the skillet and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the sauce has thickened, about 5 minutes.

Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for an additional 2 minutes. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. Serve the pork tenderloin over the tacu-tacu mixture, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired.
To make the tacu-tacu mixture, combine the cooked rice, chopped onion, chopped bell pepper, and chopped aji amarillo peppers in a large bowl. Stir to combine and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Cook the rice according to the package instructions. Set aside to cool.
Grill or broil the aji amarillo peppers until charred, about 5 minutes. Peel the peppers and chop them into small pieces.
Toast the peanuts in a small skillet over medium heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant and lightly browned.
Grate the ginger and mince the garlic using a microplane or fine grater.
